description: This figure combines engineering stress-strain curves and tensile fracture SEM characterizations for two low-density medium Mn steels (M1: Fe-8Mn-3Al-0.2C, M2: Fe-8Mn-3Al-1Si-0.2C) processed via hot rolling (HR) and intercritical annealing (IA). Subplots a and d display mechanical performance: HR samples (M1-HR, M2-HR) show brittle fracture with high ultimate tensile strength (1712±10 MPa, 1055±15 MPa) and low total elongation (14.8±0.5%, 15.4±0.8%), while IA samples (M1-IA, M2-IA) exhibit ductile behavior with moderate strength (867±10 MPa, 698±20 MPa) and significantly improved elongation (71.8±0.5%, 59.4±0.6%). Subplots b, c, e, f are 10 μm-scale SEM fractographs: HR samples present cleavage features including flat facets and river markings, while IA samples show typical dimple rupture. This figure confirms that intercritical annealing introduces stable retained austenite, enabling excellent strength-ductility matching via TRIP effect, with Si-free M1 achieving a higher product of strength and ductility (61 GPa%) than Si-containing M2 (41 GPa%).
